title: AWS SageMaker
definition: Amazon's fully managed ML platform providing end-to-end tools for building, training, and deploying ML models at scale. Includes SageMaker Studio, Training Jobs, Experiments, Pipelines, Model Monitor, and Endpoints.
why_used: Most comprehensive managed ML platform on AWS. Removes infrastructure management, provides scalable GPU training, one-click deployment, and built-in model monitoring.
interview_point: Key SageMaker concepts: Training Jobs (managed ML training on EC2), Endpoints (real-time inference), Batch Transform (offline inference on large datasets), Pipelines (MLOps automation), Feature Store (shared feature repository), and Clarify (bias and explainability). You pay only for compute used during training/inference.
real_world_example:
```python
import sagemaker
from sagemaker.sklearn.estimator import SKLearn
from sagemaker.model import Model

sess = sagemaker.Session()
role = sagemaker.get_execution_role()

# Train a scikit-learn model on SageMaker
estimator = SKLearn(
    entry_point='train.py',
    framework_version='1.0-1',
    instance_type='ml.m5.xlarge',
    role=role,
    hyperparameters={
        'n-estimators': 200,
        'max-depth': 10
    }
)

estimator.fit({'train': 's3://my-bucket/train/', 'test': 's3://my-bucket/test/'})

# Deploy to real-time endpoint
predictor = estimator.deploy(
    initial_instance_count=1,
    instance_type='ml.t2.medium'
)

# Inference
prediction = predictor.predict([[1.2, 3.4, 5.6]])

# Cleanup
predictor.delete_endpoint()
```

title: Vertex AI
definition: Google Cloud's unified ML platform combining the best of AutoML and custom training. Provides managed training, serving, pipelines, feature store, model registry, and Workbench (managed notebooks).
why_used: Best-in-class managed ML platform for GCP users. Tight integration with BigQuery, Google's TPUs, and Gemini models. AutoML provides no-code model training for common tasks.
interview_point: Vertex AI Pipelines uses Kubeflow Pipelines SDK to define ML workflows as DAGs of containerized components. Each component has defined inputs/outputs and can be cached. Vertex AI Model Monitoring automatically detects data drift in production predictions. Feature Store provides online (low latency) and offline (batch) feature serving.
real_world_example:
```python
from google.cloud import aiplatform
from google.cloud.aiplatform import pipeline_jobs

aiplatform.init(project='my-project', location='us-central1')

# Custom training job
job = aiplatform.CustomTrainingJob(
    display_name='fraud-detection-training',
    script_path='trainer/task.py',
    container_uri='gcr.io/cloud-aiplatform/training/scikit-learn-cpu.0-23:latest',
    requirements=['xgboost==1.6.0', 'pandas'],
    model_serving_container_image_uri='gcr.io/cloud-aiplatform/prediction/sklearn-cpu.0-23:latest'
)

model = job.run(
    dataset=dataset,
    model_display_name='fraud-detector-v1',
    machine_type='n1-standard-4',
    args=['--n-estimators=200', '--max-depth=6']
)

# Deploy
endpoint = model.deploy(
    machine_type='n1-standard-2',
    min_replica_count=1
)
```

title: Amazon S3
definition: Amazon's object storage service offering virtually unlimited, highly durable (99.999999999% — 11 nines) storage. Stores data as objects in buckets with a flat namespace. Standard storage for ML datasets, model artifacts, and training data.
why_used: Universal storage backbone for ML pipelines. Training data, model weights, and inference outputs all flow through S3. Integrates with all AWS services and most ML tools.
interview_point: S3 storage classes for ML cost optimization: Standard (frequent access training data), S3-IA (infrequent — older datasets), Glacier (archival — historical data). Use S3 Select to filter data server-side before downloading. S3 Transfer Acceleration for fast global uploads.
real_world_example:
```python
import boto3
import pandas as pd
from io import BytesIO
import pickle

s3 = boto3.client('s3')

# Upload training data
def upload_df(df: pd.DataFrame, bucket: str, key: str):
    buffer = BytesIO()
    df.to_parquet(buffer, index=False)
    s3.put_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=key, Body=buffer.getvalue())
    print(f'Uploaded to s3://{bucket}/{key}')

# Download and load
def load_df(bucket: str, key: str) -> pd.DataFrame:
    obj = s3.get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=key)
    return pd.read_parquet(BytesIO(obj['Body'].read()))

# Upload trained model
def save_model(model, bucket: str, key: str):
    buffer = BytesIO()
    pickle.dump(model, buffer)
    s3.put_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=key, Body=buffer.getvalue())

# Usage
upload_df(train_df, 'my-ml-bucket', 'data/train/features.parquet')
save_model(trained_model, 'my-ml-bucket', 'models/fraud_detector_v2.pkl')
```
